# Master Plan Template

Use this template to define the project-level plan. A Master Plan explains the direction of the whole effort and becomes the reference point for specific plans.

## Vision

Describe the long-term outcome the project is moving toward. State the value the project should create and the operating principle that should guide future decisions.

## Objective

Define the concrete objective for this planning cycle. The objective should be measurable enough to determine whether the plan prepared the right work.

## Scope

List the capabilities, systems, workflows, documentation, decisions, and planning outputs included in this plan.

## Non-Scope

List the work that is intentionally excluded. Non-scope protects the plan from accidental expansion and prevents implementers from assuming omitted work is implied.

## Users

Identify the people or systems that will consume the project output. Include primary users, secondary users, maintainers, implementation agents, or operational stakeholders when relevant.

## Architecture

Describe the planned architecture at the level needed for implementation preparation. Include system boundaries, major components, integration points, data ownership, and constraints.

## Risks

Describe technical, product, operational, security, schedule, and adoption risks. For each risk, explain the likely impact and the mitigation or validation strategy.

## Dependencies

List project dependencies, including prior decisions, external systems, libraries, documentation, approvals, research, people, or implementation prerequisites.

## Roadmap

Describe the planned sequence of phases or workstreams. Each roadmap item should have a clear outcome and dependency relationship.

## Success Criteria

Define what must be true for the Master Plan to be considered successful. Include planning completeness, approval state, validation readiness, and implementation handoff quality.
